objective: Extend the test-only write-set so every existing positive kernel and control fixture can inject the same explicit trusted offline authority verifier required by the implementation stage.
executable_blocker: Six existing positive test modules construct BridgeKernel or ControlRouter without an explicit verifier. A source-only compatibility path would create implicit trust and violate the original fail-closed stage contract.
preservation_contract: all original envelope constraints acceptance commands and stop conditions remain active
added files receive only explicit synthetic trusted-verifier fixture construction and call-site injection
no assertion expected value test scenario or mutation-count requirement is deleted relaxed skipped or changed
missing verifier resolver trust policy or approval remains fail closed in production source and dedicated negative-path tests
no compatibility default allow self-issued trust or test-only branch enters production source
acceptance_commands: python3 -m unittest tests.test_stage1_admission tests.test_stage1_assurance tests.test_stage1_execution_assurance tests.test_stage1_reference_vertical tests.test_stage1_control tests.test_stage1_control_assurance -v
exact write-set test-assertion skip dependency private-locator network and live-authority scan
stop_conditions: source compatibility default allow or caller self-asserted trust is introduced
any added test file changes assertions expected values scenarios or mutation counts beyond verifier fixture injection
write-set expansion contract edit new dependency or ledger domain IPC transport edit is required
missing-trust forged-issuer unresolved-policy or fabricated-approval negative path reaches mutation
rollback: Agent 0 discards the worker commit and deletes the isolated branch/worktree. The public authority amendment can be reverted independently; no runtime database contract policy source domain registry or external system is mutated.
